1→// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
     2→// Copyright Authors of Cilium
     3→
     4→//go:build linux
     5→
     6→package bpf
     7→
     8→import (
     9→	"context"
    10→	"errors"
    11→	"fmt"
    12→	"io/fs"
    13→	"iter"
    14→	"log/slog"
    15→	"math"
    16→	"os"
    17→	"path"
    18→	"reflect"
    19→	"strings"
    20→
    21→	"github.com/cilium/ebpf"
    22→	"golang.org/x/sys/unix"
    23→
    24→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/api/v1/models"
    25→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/controller"
    26→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock"
    27→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging"
    28→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields"
    29→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/metrics"
    30→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/option"
    31→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/spanstat"
    32→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/time"
    33→)
    34→
    35→var (
    36→	// ErrMaxLookup is returned when the maximum number of map element lookups has
    37→	// been reached.
    38→	ErrMaxLookup = errors.New("maximum number of lookups reached")
    39→
    40→	bpfMapSyncControllerGroup = controller.NewGroup("bpf-map-sync")
    41→)
    42→
    43→type MapKey interface {
    44→	fmt.Stringer
    45→
    46→	// New must return a pointer to a new MapKey.
    47→	New() MapKey
    48→}
    49→
    50→type MapValue interface {
    51→	fmt.Stringer
    52→
    53→	// New must return a pointer to a new MapValue.
    54→	New() MapValue
    55→}
    56→
    57→// MapPerCPUValue is the same as MapValue, but for per-CPU maps. Implement to be
    58→// able to fetch map values from all CPUs.
    59→type MapPerCPUValue interface {
    60→	MapValue
    61→
    62→	// NewSlice must return a pointer to a slice of structs that implement MapValue.
    63→	NewSlice() any
    64→}
    65→
    66→type cacheEntry struct {
    67→	Key   MapKey
    68→	Value MapValue
    69→
    70→	DesiredAction DesiredAction
    71→	LastError     error
    72→}
    73→
    74→type Map struct {
    75→	Logger *slog.Logger
    76→	m      *ebpf.Map
    77→	// spec will be nil after the map has been created
    78→	spec *ebpf.MapSpec
    79→
    80→	key   MapKey
    81→	value MapValue
    82→
    83→	name string
    84→	path string
    85→	lock lock.RWMutex
    86→
    87→	// cachedCommonName is the common portion of the name excluding any
    88→	// endpoint ID
    89→	cachedCommonName string
    90→
    91→	// enableSync is true when synchronization retries have been enabled.
    92→	enableSync bool
    93→
    94→	// withValueCache is true when map cache has been enabled
    95→	withValueCache bool
    96→
    97→	// cache as key/value entries when map cache is enabled or as key-only when
    98→	// pressure metric is enabled
    99→	cache map[string]*cacheEntry
   100→
   101→	// errorResolverLastScheduled is the timestamp when the error resolver
   102→	// was last scheduled
   103→	errorResolverLastScheduled time.Time
   104→
   105→	// outstandingErrors states whether there are outstanding errors, occurred while
   106→	// syncing an entry with the kernel, that need to be resolved. This variable exists
   107→	// to avoid iterating over the full cache to check if reconciliation is necessary,
   108→	// but it is possible that it gets out of sync if an error is automatically
   109→	// resolved while performing a subsequent Update/Delete operation on the same key.
   110→	outstandingErrors bool
   111→
   112→	// pressureGauge is a metric that tracks the pressure on this map
   113→	pressureGauge *metrics.GaugeWithThreshold
   114→
   115→	// is true when events buffer is enabled.
   116→	eventsBufferEnabled bool
   117→
   118→	// contains optional event buffer which stores last n bpf map events.
   119→	events *eventsBuffer
   120→
   121→	// group is the metric group name for this map, it classifies maps of the same
   122→	// type that share the same metric group.
   123→	group string
   124→}
   125→
   126→func (m *Map) Type() ebpf.MapType {
   127→	if m.m != nil {
   128→		return m.m.Type()
   129→	}
   130→	if m.spec != nil {
   131→		return m.spec.Type
   132→	}
   133→	return ebpf.UnspecifiedMap
   134→}
   135→
   136→type nopDecoder []struct{}
   137→
   138→func (nopDecoder) UnmarshalBinary(data []byte) error {
   139→	return nil
   140→}
   141→
   142→// BatchCount the number of elements in the map using a batch lookup.
   143→// Only usable for hash, lru-hash and lpm-trie maps.
   144→func (m *Map) BatchCount() (count int, err error) {
   145→	switch m.Type() {
   146→	case ebpf.Hash, ebpf.LRUHash, ebpf.LPMTrie:
   147→		break
   148→	default:
   149→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("unsupported map type %s, must be one either hash or lru-hash types", m.Type())
   150→	}
   151→	chunkSize := startingChunkSize(int(m.MaxEntries()))
   152→
   153→	// Since we don't care about the actual data we just use a no-op binary
   154→	// decoder.
   155→	keys := make(nopDecoder, chunkSize)
   156→	vals := make(nopDecoder, chunkSize)
   157→	maxRetries := defaultBatchedRetries
   158→
   159→	var cursor ebpf.MapBatchCursor
   160→	for {
   161→		for retry := range maxRetries {
   162→			// Attempt to read batch into buffer.
   163→			c, batchErr := m.BatchLookup(&cursor, keys, vals, nil)
   164→			count += c
   165→
   166→			switch {
   167→			// Lookup batch on LRU hash map may fail if the buffer passed is not big enough to
   168→			// accommodate the largest bucket size in the LRU map. See full comment in
   169→			// [BatchIterator.IterateAll]
   170→			case errors.Is(batchErr, unix.ENOSPC):
   171→				if retry == maxRetries-1 {
   172→					err = batchErr
   173→				} else {
   174→					chunkSize *= 2
   175→				}
   176→				keys = make(nopDecoder, chunkSize)
   177→				vals = make(nopDecoder, chunkSize)
   178→				continue
   179→			case errors.Is(batchErr, ebpf.ErrKeyNotExist):
   180→				return
   181→			case batchErr != nil:
   182→				// If we're not done, and we didn't hit a ENOSPC then stop iteration and record
   183→				// the error.
   184→				err = fmt.Errorf("failed to iterate map: %w", batchErr)
   185→				return
   186→			}
   187→			// Do the next batch
   188→			break
   189→		}
   190→	}
   191→}
   192→
   193→func (m *Map) KeySize() uint32 {
   194→	if m.m != nil {
   195→		return m.m.KeySize()
   196→	}
   197→	if m.spec != nil {
   198→		return m.spec.KeySize
   199→	}
   200→	return 0
   201→}
   202→
   203→func (m *Map) ValueSize() uint32 {
   204→	if m.m != nil {
   205→		return m.m.ValueSize()
   206→	}
   207→	if m.spec != nil {
   208→		return m.spec.ValueSize
   209→	}
   210→	return 0
   211→}
   212→
   213→func (m *Map) MaxEntries() uint32 {
   214→	if m.m != nil {
   215→		return m.m.MaxEntries()
   216→	}
   217→	if m.spec != nil {
   218→		return m.spec.MaxEntries
   219→	}
   220→	return 0
   221→}
   222→
   223→func (m *Map) Flags() uint32 {
   224→	if m.m != nil {
   225→		return m.m.Flags()
   226→	}
   227→	if m.spec != nil {
   228→		return m.spec.Flags
   229→	}
   230→	return 0
   231→}
   232→
   233→func (m *Map) hasPerCPUValue() bool {
   234→	mt := m.Type()
   235→	return mt == ebpf.PerCPUHash || mt == ebpf.PerCPUArray || mt == ebpf.LRUCPUHash || mt == ebpf.PerCPUCGroupStorage
   236→}
   237→
   238→func (m *Map) updateMetrics() {
   239→	if m.group == "" {
   240→		return
   241→	}
   242→	metrics.UpdateMapCapacity(m.group, m.MaxEntries())
   243→}
   244→
   245→// NewMap creates a new Map instance - object representing a BPF map
   246→func NewMap(name string, mapType ebpf.MapType, mapKey MapKey, mapValue MapValue,
   247→	maxEntries int, flags uint32) *Map {
   248→
   249→	// slogloggercheck: it's safe to use the default logger here as it has been initialized by the program up to this point.
   250→	defaultSlogLogger := logging.DefaultSlogLogger
   251→	keySize := reflect.TypeOf(mapKey).Elem().Size()
   252→	valueSize := reflect.TypeOf(mapValue).Elem().Size()
   253→
   254→	return &Map{
   255→		Logger: defaultSlogLogger.With(
   256→			logfields.BPFMapPath, name,
   257→			logfields.BPFMapName, name,
   258→		),
   259→		spec: &ebpf.MapSpec{
   260→			Type:       mapType,
   261→			Name:       path.Base(name),
   262→			KeySize:    uint32(keySize),
   263→			ValueSize:  uint32(valueSize),
   264→			MaxEntries: uint32(maxEntries),
   265→			Flags:      flags,
   266→		},
   267→		name:  path.Base(name),
   268→		key:   mapKey,
   269→		value: mapValue,
   270→		group: name,
   271→	}
   272→}
   273→
   274→// NewMapWithInnerSpec creates a new Map instance - object representing a BPF map with an inner map specification
   275→func NewMapWithInnerSpec(name string, mapType ebpf.MapType, mapKey MapKey, mapValue MapValue,
   276→	maxEntries int, flags uint32, innerSpec *ebpf.MapSpec) *Map {
   277→
   278→	// slogloggercheck: it's safe to use the default logger here as it has been initialized by the program up to this point.
   279→	defaultSlogLogger := logging.DefaultSlogLogger
   280→	keySize := reflect.TypeOf(mapKey).Elem().Size()
   281→	valueSize := reflect.TypeOf(mapValue).Elem().Size()
   282→
   283→	return &Map{
   284→		Logger: defaultSlogLogger.With(
   285→			logfields.BPFMapPath, name,
   286→			logfields.BPFMapName, name,
   287→		),
   288→		spec: &ebpf.MapSpec{
   289→			Type:       mapType,
   290→			Name:       path.Base(name),
   291→			KeySize:    uint32(keySize),
   292→			ValueSize:  uint32(valueSize),
   293→			MaxEntries: uint32(maxEntries),
   294→			Flags:      flags,
   295→			InnerMap:   innerSpec,
   296→		},
   297→		name:  path.Base(name),
   298→		key:   mapKey,
   299→		value: mapValue,
   300→	}
   301→}
   302→
   303→func (m *Map) commonName() string {
   304→	if m.cachedCommonName != "" {
   305→		return m.cachedCommonName
   306→	}
   307→
   308→	m.cachedCommonName = extractCommonName(m.name)
   309→	return m.cachedCommonName
   310→}
   311→
   312→func (m *Map) NonPrefixedName() string {
   313→	return strings.TrimPrefix(m.name, metrics.Namespace+"_")
   314→}
   315→
   316→// scheduleErrorResolver schedules a periodic resolver controller that scans
   317→// all BPF map caches for unresolved errors and attempts to resolve them. On
   318→// error of resolution, the controller is-rescheduled in an expedited manner
   319→// with an exponential back-off.
   320→//
   321→// m.lock must be held for writing
   322→func (m *Map) scheduleErrorResolver() {
   323→	m.outstandingErrors = true
   324→
   325→	if time.Since(m.errorResolverLastScheduled) <= errorResolverSchedulerMinInterval {
   326→		return
   327→	}
   328→
   329→	m.errorResolverLastScheduled = time.Now()
   330→
   331→	go func() {
   332→		time.Sleep(errorResolverSchedulerDelay)
   333→		mapControllers.UpdateController(m.controllerName(),
   334→			controller.ControllerParams{
   335→				Group:       bpfMapSyncControllerGroup,
   336→				DoFunc:      m.resolveErrors,
   337→				RunInterval: errorResolverSchedulerMinInterval,
   338→			},
   339→		)
   340→	}()
   341→
   342→}
   343→
   344→// WithCache enables use of a cache. This will store all entries inserted from
   345→// user space in a local cache (map) and will indicate the status of each
   346→// individual entry.
   347→func (m *Map) WithCache() *Map {
   348→	if m.cache == nil {
   349→		m.cache = map[string]*cacheEntry{}
   350→	}
   351→	m.withValueCache = true
   352→	m.enableSync = true
   353→	return m
   354→}
   355→
   356→// WithEvents enables use of the event buffer, if the buffer is enabled.
   357→// This stores all map events (i.e. add/update/delete) in a bounded event buffer.
   358→// If eventTTL is not zero, then events that are older than the TTL
   359→// will periodically be removed from the buffer.
   360→// Enabling events will use approx proportional to 100MB for every million capacity
   361→// in maxSize.
   362→//
   363→// TODO: The IPCache map have many periodic update events added by a controller for entries such as the 0.0.0.0/0 range.
   364→// These fill the event buffer with possibly unnecessary events.
   365→// We should either provide an option to aggregate these events, ignore hem from the ipcache event buffer or store them in a separate buffer.
   366→func (m *Map) WithEvents(c option.BPFEventBufferConfig) *Map {
   367→	if !c.Enabled {
   368→		return m
   369→	}
   370→	m.Logger.Debug(
   371→		"enabling events buffer",
   372→		logfields.Size, c.MaxSize,
   373→		logfields.TTL, c.TTL,
   374→	)
   375→	m.eventsBufferEnabled = true
   376→	m.initEventsBuffer(c.MaxSize, c.TTL)
   377→	return m
   378→}
   379→
   380→func (m *Map) WithGroupName(group string) *Map {
   381→	m.group = group
   382→	return m
   383→}
   384→
   385→// WithPressureMetricThreshold enables the tracking of a metric that measures
   386→// the pressure of this map. This metric is only reported if over the
   387→// threshold.
   388→func (m *Map) WithPressureMetricThreshold(registry *metrics.Registry, threshold float64) *Map {
   389→	if registry == nil {
   390→		return m
   391→	}
   392→
   393→	// When pressure metric is enabled, we keep track of map keys in cache
   394→	if m.cache == nil {
   395→		m.cache = map[string]*cacheEntry{}
   396→	}
   397→
   398→	m.pressureGauge = registry.NewBPFMapPressureGauge(m.NonPrefixedName(), threshold)
   399→
   400→	return m
   401→}
   402→
   403→// WithPressureMetric enables tracking and reporting of this map pressure with
   404→// threshold 0.
   405→func (m *Map) WithPressureMetric(registry *metrics.Registry) *Map {
   406→	return m.WithPressureMetricThreshold(registry, 0.0)
   407→}
   408→
   409→// UpdatePressureMetricWithSize updates map pressure metric using the given map size.
   410→func (m *Map) UpdatePressureMetricWithSize(size int32) {
   411→	if m.pressureGauge == nil {
   412→		return
   413→	}
   414→
   415→	// Do a lazy check of MetricsConfig as it is not available at map static
   416→	// initialization.
   417→	if !metrics.BPFMapPressure {
   418→		if !m.withValueCache {
   419→			m.cache = nil
   420→		}
   421→		m.pressureGauge = nil
   422→		return
   423→	}
   424→
   425→	pvalue := float64(size) / float64(m.MaxEntries())
   426→	m.pressureGauge.Set(pvalue)
   427→}
   428→
   429→func (m *Map) updatePressureMetric() {
   430→	// Skipping pressure metric gauge updates for LRU map as the cache size
   431→	// does not accurately represent the actual map size.
   432→	if m.spec != nil && m.spec.Type == ebpf.LRUHash {
   433→		return
   434→	}
   435→	m.UpdatePressureMetricWithSize(int32(len(m.cache)))
   436→}
   437→
   438→func (m *Map) FD() int {
   439→	return m.m.FD()
   440→}
   441→
   442→// Name returns the basename of this map.
   443→func (m *Map) Name() string {
   444→	return m.name
   445→}
   446→
   447→// Path returns the path to this map on the filesystem.
   448→func (m *Map) Path() (string, error) {
   449→	if err := m.setPathIfUnset(); err != nil {
   450→		return "", err
   451→	}
   452→
   453→	return m.path, nil
   454→}
   455→
   456→// Unpin attempts to unpin (remove) the map from the filesystem.
   457→func (m *Map) Unpin() error {
   458→	path, err := m.Path()
   459→	if err != nil {
   460→		return err
   461→	}
   462→
   463→	return os.RemoveAll(path)
   464→}
   465→
   466→// UnpinIfExists tries to unpin (remove) the map only if it exists.
   467→func (m *Map) UnpinIfExists() error {
   468→	found, err := m.exist()
   469→	if err != nil {
   470→		return err
   471→	}
   472→
   473→	if !found {
   474→		return nil
   475→	}
   476→
   477→	return m.Unpin()
   478→}
   479→
   480→func (m *Map) controllerName() string {
   481→	return fmt.Sprintf("bpf-map-sync-%s", m.name)
   482→}
   483→
   484→// OpenMap opens the map at pinPath.
   485→func OpenMap(pinPath string, key MapKey, value MapValue) (*Map, error) {
   486→	if !path.IsAbs(pinPath) {
   487→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("pinPath must be absolute: %s", pinPath)
   488→	}
   489→
   490→	em, err := ebpf.LoadPinnedMap(pinPath, nil)
   491→	if err != nil {
   492→		return nil, err
   493→	}
   494→
   495→	// slogloggercheck: it's safe to use the default logger here as it has been initialized by the program up to this point.
   496→	defaultSlogLogger := logging.DefaultSlogLogger
   497→
   498→	logger := defaultSlogLogger.With(
   499→		logfields.BPFMapPath, pinPath,
   500→		logfields.BPFMapName, path.Base(pinPath),
   501→	)
   502→	m := &Map{
   503→		Logger: logger,
   504→		m:      em,
   505→		name:   path.Base(pinPath),
   506→		path:   pinPath,
   507→		key:    key,
   508→		value:  value,
   509→	}
   510→
   511→	m.updateMetrics()
   512→	registerMap(logger, pinPath, m)
   513→
   514→	return m, nil
   515→}
   516→
   517→func (m *Map) setPathIfUnset() error {
   518→	if m.path == "" {
   519→		if m.name == "" {
   520→			return fmt.Errorf("either path or name must be set")
   521→		}
   522→
   523→		m.path = MapPath(m.Logger, m.name)
   524→	}
   525→
   526→	return nil
   527→}
   528→
   529→// Recreate removes any pin at the Map's pin path, recreates and re-pins it.
   530→func (m *Map) Recreate() error {
   531→	m.lock.Lock()
   532→	defer m.lock.Unlock()
   533→
   534→	if m.m != nil {
   535→		return fmt.Errorf("map already open: %s", m.name)
   536→	}
   537→
   538→	if err := m.setPathIfUnset(); err != nil {
   539→		return err
   540→	}
   541→
   542→	if err := os.Remove(m.path); err != nil && !errors.Is(err, fs.ErrNotExist) {
   543→		return fmt.Errorf("removing pinned map %s: %w", m.name, err)
   544→	}
   545→
   546→	m.Logger.Info(
   547→		"Removed map pin, recreating and re-pinning map",
   548→	)
   549→
   550→	return m.openOrCreate(true)
   551→}
   552→
   553→// IsOpen returns true if the map has been opened.
   554→func (m *Map) IsOpen() bool {
   555→	m.lock.Lock()
   556→	defer m.lock.Unlock()
   557→	return m.m != nil
   558→}
   559→
   560→// OpenOrCreate attempts to open the Map, or if it does not yet exist, create
   561→// the Map. If the existing map's attributes such as map type, key/value size,
   562→// capacity, etc. do not match the Map's attributes, then the map will be
   563→// deleted and reopened without any attempt to retain its previous contents.
   564→// If the map is marked as non-persistent, it will always be recreated.
   565→//
   566→// Returns whether the map was deleted and recreated, or an optional error.
   567→func (m *Map) OpenOrCreate() error {
   568→	m.lock.Lock()
   569→	defer m.lock.Unlock()
   570→
   571→	return m.openOrCreate(true)
   572→}
   573→
   574→// CreateUnpinned creates the map without pinning it to the file system.
   575→//
   576→// TODO(tb): Remove this when all map creation takes MapSpec.
   577→func (m *Map) CreateUnpinned() error {
   578→	m.lock.Lock()
   579→	defer m.lock.Unlock()
   580→
   581→	return m.openOrCreate(false)
   582→}
   583→
   584→// Create is similar to OpenOrCreate, but closes the map after creating or
   585→// opening it.
   586→func (m *Map) Create() error {
   587→	if err := m.OpenOrCreate(); err != nil {
   588→		return err
   589→	}
   590→	return m.Close()
   591→}
   592→
   593→func (m *Map) openOrCreate(pin bool) error {
   594→	if m.m != nil {
   595→		return nil
   596→	}
   597→
   598→	if m.spec == nil {
   599→		return fmt.Errorf("attempted to create map %s without MapSpec", m.name)
   600→	}
   601→
   602→	if err := m.setPathIfUnset(); err != nil {
   603→		return err
   604→	}
   605→
   606→	m.spec.Flags |= GetMapMemoryFlags(m.spec.Type)
   607→
   608→	if m.spec.InnerMap != nil {
   609→		m.spec.InnerMap.Flags |= GetMapMemoryFlags(m.spec.InnerMap.Type)
   610→	}
   611→
   612→	if pin {
   613→		m.spec.Pinning = ebpf.PinByName
   614→	}
   615→
   616→	em, err := OpenOrCreateMap(m.Logger, m.spec, path.Dir(m.path))
   617→	if err != nil {
   618→		return err
   619→	}
   620→
   621→	m.updateMetrics()
   622→	registerMap(m.Logger, m.path, m)
   623→
   624→	// Consume the MapSpec.
   625→	m.spec = nil
   626→
   627→	// Retain the Map.
   628→	m.m = em
   629→
   630→	return nil
   631→}
   632→
   633→// Open opens the BPF map. All calls to Open() are serialized due to acquiring
   634→// m.lock
   635→func (m *Map) Open() error {
   636→	m.lock.Lock()
   637→	defer m.lock.Unlock()
   638→
   639→	return m.open()
   640→}
   641→
   642→// open opens the BPF map. It is identical to Open() but should be used when
   643→// m.lock is already held. open() may only be used if m.lock is held for
   644→// writing.
   645→func (m *Map) open() error {
   646→	if m.m != nil {
   647→		return nil
   648→	}
   649→
   650→	if err := m.setPathIfUnset(); err != nil {
   651→		return err
   652→	}
   653→
   654→	em, err := ebpf.LoadPinnedMap(m.path, nil)
   655→	if err != nil {
   656→		return fmt.Errorf("loading pinned map %s: %w", m.path, err)
   657→	}
   658→
   659→	m.updateMetrics()
   660→	registerMap(m.Logger, m.path, m)
   661→
   662→	m.m = em
   663→
   664→	return nil
   665→}
   666→
   667→func (m *Map) Close() error {
   668→	m.lock.Lock()
   669→	defer m.lock.Unlock()
   670→
   671→	if m.enableSync {
   672→		mapControllers.RemoveController(m.controllerName())
   673→	}
   674→
   675→	if m.m != nil {
   676→		m.m.Close()
   677→		m.m = nil
   678→	}
   679→
   680→	unregisterMap(m.Logger, m.path, m)
   681→
   682→	return nil
   683→}
   684→
   685→func (m *Map) NextKey(key, nextKeyOut any) error {
   686→	var duration *spanstat.SpanStat
   687→	if metrics.BPFSyscallDuration.IsEnabled() {
   688→		duration = spanstat.Start()
   689→	}
   690→
   691→	err := m.m.NextKey(key, nextKeyOut)
   692→
   693→	if metrics.BPFSyscallDuration.IsEnabled() {
   694→		metrics.BPFSyscallDuration.WithLabelValues(metricOpGetNextKey, metrics.Error2Outcome(err)).Observe(duration.End(err == nil).Total().Seconds())
   695→	}
   696→
   697→	return err
   698→}
   699→
   700→type DumpCallback func(key MapKey, value MapValue)
   701→
   702→// DumpWithCallback iterates over the Map and calls the given DumpCallback for
   703→// each map entry. With the current implementation, it is safe for callbacks to
   704→// retain the values received, as they are guaranteed to be new instances.
   705→//
   706→// To dump per-cpu maps, use DumpPerCPUWithCallback.
   707→func (m *Map) DumpWithCallback(cb DumpCallback) error {
   708→	if cb == nil {
   709→		return errors.New("empty callback")
   710→	}
   711→
   712→	if err := m.Open(); err != nil {
   713→		return err
   714→	}
   715→
   716→	m.lock.RLock()
   717→	defer m.lock.RUnlock()
   718→
   719→	// Don't need deep copies here, only fresh pointers.
   720→	mk := m.key.New()
   721→	mv := m.value.New()
   722→
   723→	i := m.m.Iterate()
   724→	for i.Next(mk, mv) {
   725→		cb(mk, mv)
   726→
   727→		mk = m.key.New()
   728→		mv = m.value.New()
   729→	}
   730→
   731→	return i.Err()
   732→}
   733→
   734→// DumpPerCPUCallback is called by DumpPerCPUWithCallback with the map key and
   735→// the slice of all values from all CPUs.
   736→type DumpPerCPUCallback func(key MapKey, values any)
   737→
   738→// DumpPerCPUWithCallback iterates over the Map and calls the given
   739→// DumpPerCPUCallback for each map entry, passing the slice with all values
   740→// from all CPUs. With the current implementation, it is safe for callbacks
   741→// to retain the values received, as they are guaranteed to be new instances.
   742→func (m *Map) DumpPerCPUWithCallback(cb DumpPerCPUCallback) error {
   743→	if cb == nil {
   744→		return errors.New("empty callback")
   745→	}
   746→
   747→	if !m.hasPerCPUValue() {
   748→		return fmt.Errorf("map %s is not a per-CPU map", m.name)
   749→	}
   750→
   751→	v, ok := m.value.(MapPerCPUValue)
   752→	if !ok {
   753→		return fmt.Errorf("map %s value type does not implement MapPerCPUValue", m.name)
   754→	}
   755→
   756→	if err := m.Open(); err != nil {
   757→		return err
   758→	}
   759→
   760→	m.lock.RLock()
   761→	defer m.lock.RUnlock()
   762→
   763→	// Don't need deep copies here, only fresh pointers.
   764→	mk := m.key.New()
   765→	mv := v.NewSlice()
   766→
   767→	i := m.m.Iterate()
   768→	for i.Next(mk, mv) {
   769→		cb(mk, mv)
   770→
   771→		mk = m.key.New()
   772→		mv = v.NewSlice()
   773→	}
   774→
   775→	return i.Err()
   776→}
   777→
   778→// DumpWithCallbackIfExists is similar to DumpWithCallback, but returns earlier
   779→// if the given map does not exist.
   780→func (m *Map) DumpWithCallbackIfExists(cb DumpCallback) error {
   781→	found, err := m.exist()
   782→	if err != nil {
   783→		return err
   784→	}
   785→
   786→	if found {
   787→		return m.DumpWithCallback(cb)
   788→	}
   789→
   790→	return nil
   791→}
   792→
   793→// DumpReliablyWithCallback is similar to DumpWithCallback, but performs
   794→// additional tracking of the current and recently seen keys, so that if an
   795→// element is removed from the underlying kernel map during the dump, the dump
   796→// can continue from a recently seen key rather than restarting from scratch.
   797→// In addition, it caps the maximum number of map entry iterations at 4 times
   798→// the maximum map size. If this limit is reached, ErrMaxLookup is returned.
   799→//
   800→// The caller must provide a callback for handling each entry, and a stats
   801→// object initialized via a call to NewDumpStats(). The callback function must
   802→// not invoke any map operations that acquire the Map.lock.
   803→func (m *Map) DumpReliablyWithCallback(cb DumpCallback, stats *DumpStats) error {
   804→	if cb == nil {
   805→		return errors.New("empty callback")
   806→	}
   807→
   808→	if stats == nil {
   809→		return errors.New("stats is nil")
   810→	}
   811→
   812→	var (
   813→		prevKey    = m.key.New()
   814→		currentKey = m.key.New()
   815→		nextKey    = m.key.New()
   816→		value      = m.value.New()
   817→
   818→		prevKeyValid = false
   819→	)
   820→
   821→	stats.start()
   822→	defer stats.finish()
   823→
   824→	if err := m.Open(); err != nil {
   825→		return err
   826→	}
   827→
   828→	// Acquire a (write) lock here as callers can invoke map operations in the
   829→	// DumpCallback that need a (write) lock.
   830→	// See PR for more details. - https://github.com/cilium/cilium/pull/38590.
   831→	m.lock.Lock()
   832→	defer m.lock.Unlock()
   833→	if m.m == nil {
   834→		// We currently don't prevent open maps from being closed.
   835→		// See GH issue - https://github.com/cilium/cilium/issues/39287.
   836→		return errors.New("map is closed")
   837→	}
   838→
   839→	// Get the first map key.
   840→	if err := m.NextKey(nil, currentKey); err != nil {
   841→		stats.Lookup = 1
   842→		if errors.Is(err, ebpf.ErrKeyNotExist) {
   843→			// Empty map, nothing to iterate.
   844→			stats.Completed = true
   845→			return nil
   846→		}
   847→	}
   848→
   849→	// maxLookup is an upper bound limit to prevent backtracking forever
   850→	// when iterating over the map's elements (the map might be concurrently
   851→	// updated while being iterated)
   852→	maxLookup := stats.MaxEntries * 4
   853→
   854→	// This loop stops when all elements have been iterated (Map.NextKey() returns
   855→	// ErrKeyNotExist) OR, in order to avoid hanging if
   856→	// the map is continuously updated, when maxLookup has been reached
   857→	for stats.Lookup = 1; stats.Lookup <= maxLookup; stats.Lookup++ {
   858→		// currentKey was set by the first m.NextKey() above. We know it existed in
   859→		// the map, but it may have been deleted by a concurrent map operation.
   860→		//
   861→		// If currentKey is no longer in the map, nextKey may be the first key in
   862→		// the map again. Continue with nextKey only if we still find currentKey in
   863→		// the Lookup() after the call to m.NextKey(), this way we know nextKey is
   864→		// NOT the first key in the map and iteration hasn't reset.
   865→		nextKeyErr := m.NextKey(currentKey, nextKey)
   866→
   867→		if err := m.m.Lookup(currentKey, value); err != nil {
   868→			stats.LookupFailed++
   869→			// Restarting from a invalid key starts the iteration again from the beginning.
   870→			// If we have a previously found key, try to restart from there instead
   871→			if prevKeyValid {
   872→				currentKey = prevKey
   873→				// Restart from a given previous key only once, otherwise if the prevKey is
   874→				// concurrently deleted we might loop forever trying to look it up.
   875→				prevKeyValid = false
   876→				stats.KeyFallback++
   877→			} else {
   878→				// Depending on exactly when currentKey was deleted from the
   879→				// map, nextKey may be the actual key element after the deleted
   880→				// one, or the first element in the map.
   881→				currentKey = nextKey
   882→				// To avoid having nextKey and currentKey pointing at the same memory
   883→				// we allocate a new key for nextKey. Without this currentKey and nextKey
   884→				// would be the same pointer value and would get double iterated on the next
   885→				// iterations m.NextKey(...) call.
   886→				nextKey = m.key.New()
   887→				stats.Interrupted++
   888→			}
   889→			continue
   890→		}
   891→
   892→		cb(currentKey, value)
   893→
   894→		if nextKeyErr != nil {
   895→			if errors.Is(nextKeyErr, ebpf.ErrKeyNotExist) {
   896→				stats.Completed = true
   897→				return nil // end of map, we're done iterating
   898→			}
   899→			return nextKeyErr
   900→		}
   901→
   902→		// Prepare keys to move to the next iteration.
   903→		prevKey = currentKey
   904→		currentKey = nextKey
   905→		nextKey = m.key.New()
   906→		prevKeyValid = true
   907→	}
   908→
   909→	return ErrMaxLookup
   910→}
   911→
   912→// BatchIterator provides a typed wrapper *Map that allows for batched iteration
   913→// of bpf maps.
   914→type BatchIterator[KT, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]] struct {
   915→	m    *Map
   916→	err  error
   917→	keys []KT
   918→	vals []VT
   919→
   920→	chunkSize      int
   921→	maxDumpRetries uint32
   922→
   923→	// Iteration stats
   924→	batchSize int
   925→
   926→	opts *ebpf.BatchOptions
   927→}
   928→
   929→// NewBatchIterator that allows for iterating a map using the bpf batch api.
   930→// This automatically handles concerns such as batch sizing and handling errors
   931→// when end of map is reached.
   932→// Following iteration, any unresolved errors encountered when iterating
   933→// the bpf map can be accessed via the Err() function.
   934→// The pointer type of KT & VT must implement Map{Key,Value}, respectively.
   935→//
   936→// Subsequent iterations via IterateAll reset all internal state and begin
   937→// iteration over.
   938→//
   939→// Example usage:
   940→//
   941→//	m := NewMap("cilium_test",
   942→//		ebpf.Hash,
   943→//		&TestKey{}, 	// *TestKey implements MapKey.
   944→//		&TestValue{}, 	// *TestValue implements MapValue.
   945→//		mapSize,
   946→//		BPF_F_NO_PREALLOC,
   947→//	)
   948→//
   949→//	iter := NewBatchIterator[TestKey, TestValue](m)
   950→//	for k, v := range iter.IterateAll(context.TODO()) {
   951→//		// ...
   952→//	}
   953→func NewBatchIterator[KT any, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]](m *Map) *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP] {
   954→	return &BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]{
   955→		m: m,
   956→	}
   957→}
   958→
   959→// KeyPointer is a generic interface that provides the constraint that the pointer
   960→// of a type T is a MapKey.
   961→type KeyPointer[T any] interface {
   962→	MapKey
   963→	*T
   964→}
   965→
   966→// ValuePointer is a generic interface that provides the constraint that the pointer
   967→// of a type T is a MapValue.
   968→type ValuePointer[T any] interface {
   969→	MapValue
   970→	*T
   971→}
   972→
   973→// Err returns errors encountered during the previous iteration when
   974→// IterateAll(...) is called.
   975→//
   976→// If the iterator is reused, the error will be reset,
   977→func (kvs BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) Err() error {
   978→	return kvs.err
   979→}
   980→
   981→func (bi BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) maxBatchedRetries() int {
   982→	if bi.maxDumpRetries > 0 {
   983→		return int(bi.maxDumpRetries)
   984→	}
   985→	return defaultBatchedRetries
   986→}
   987→
   988→const defaultBatchedRetries = 3
   989→
   990→type BatchIteratorOpt[KT any, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]] func(*BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]
   991→
   992→// WithEBPFBatchOpts returns a batch iterator option that allows for overriding
   993→// BPF_MAP_LOOKUP_BATCH options.
   994→func WithEBPFBatchOpts[KT, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]](opts *ebpf.BatchOptions) BatchIteratorOpt[KT, VT, KP, VP] {
   995→	return func(in *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP] {
   996→		in.opts = opts
   997→		return in
   998→	}
   999→}
  1000→
  1001→// WithMaxRetries returns a batch iterator option that allows overriding the default
  1002→// max batch retries.
  1003→//
  1004→// Unless the starting chunk size is set to be the map size, it is possible for iteration
  1005→// to fail with ENOSPC if the passed allocated chunk array size is not big enough to accommodate
  1006→// a the bpf maps underlying hashmaps bucket size.
  1007→//
  1008→// If this happens, BatchIterator will automatically attempt to double the batch size and
  1009→// retry the iteration from the same place - up to a number of retries.
  1010→func WithMaxRetries[KT, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]](retries uint32) BatchIteratorOpt[KT, VT, KP, VP] {
  1011→	return func(in *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP] {
  1012→		in.maxDumpRetries = retries
  1013→		return in
  1014→	}
  1015→}
  1016→
  1017→// WithStartingChunkSize returns a batch iterator option that allows overriding the dynamically
  1018→// chosen starting chunk size.
  1019→func WithStartingChunkSize[KT, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]](size int) BatchIteratorOpt[KT, VT, KP, VP] {
  1020→	return func(in *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP] {
  1021→		in.chunkSize = size
  1022→		if in.chunkSize <= 0 {
  1023→			in.chunkSize = 8
  1024→		}
  1025→		return in
  1026→	}
  1027→}
  1028→
  1029→// CountAll is a helper function that returns the count of all elements in a batched
  1030→// iterator.
  1031→func CountAll[KT, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]](ctx context.Context, iter *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) (int, error) {
  1032→	c := 0
  1033→	for range iter.IterateAll(ctx) {
  1034→		c++
  1035→	}
  1036→	return c, iter.Err()
  1037→}
  1038→
  1039→func startingChunkSize(maxEntries int) int {
  1040→	bucketSize := math.Sqrt(float64(maxEntries * 2))
  1041→	nearest2 := math.Log2(bucketSize)
  1042→	return int(math.Pow(2, math.Ceil(nearest2)))
  1043→}
  1044→
  1045→// IterateAll returns an iterate Seq2 type which can be used to iterate a map
  1046→// using the batched API.
  1047→// In the case of a the iteration failing due to insufficient batch buffer size,
  1048→// this will attempt to grow the buffer by a factor of 2 (up to a default: 3 amount
  1049→// of retries) and re-attempt the iteration.
  1050→// If the number of failures exceeds max retries, then iteration will stop and an error
  1051→// will be returned via Err().
  1052→//
  1053→// All other errors will result in immediate termination of iterator.
  1054→//
  1055→// If the iteration fails, then the Err() function will return the error that caused the failure.
  1056→func (bi *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) IterateAll(ctx context.Context, opts ...BatchIteratorOpt[KT, VT, KP, VP]) iter.Seq2[KP, VP] {
  1057→	switch bi.m.Type() {
  1058→	case ebpf.Hash, ebpf.LRUHash, ebpf.LPMTrie:
  1059→		break
  1060→	default:
  1061→		bi.err = fmt.Errorf("unsupported map type %s, must be one either hash or lru-hash types", bi.m.Type())
  1062→		return func(yield func(KP, VP) bool) {}
  1063→	}
  1064→
  1065→	bi.chunkSize = startingChunkSize(int(bi.m.MaxEntries()))
  1066→
  1067→	for _, opt := range opts {
  1068→		if opt != nil {
  1069→			bi = opt(bi)
  1070→		}
  1071→	}
  1072→
  1073→	// reset values
  1074→	bi.err = nil
  1075→	bi.batchSize = 0
  1076→	bi.keys = make([]KT, bi.chunkSize)
  1077→	bi.vals = make([]VT, bi.chunkSize)
  1078→
  1079→	processed := 0
  1080→	var cursor ebpf.MapBatchCursor
  1081→	return func(yield func(KP, VP) bool) {
  1082→		if bi.Err() != nil {
  1083→			return
  1084→		}
  1085→
  1086→	iterate:
  1087→		for {
  1088→			if ctx.Err() != nil {
  1089→				bi.err = ctx.Err()
  1090→				return
  1091→			}
  1092→		retry:
  1093→			for retry := range bi.maxBatchedRetries() {
  1094→				// Attempt to read batch into buffer.
  1095→				c, batchErr := bi.m.BatchLookup(&cursor, bi.keys, bi.vals, nil)
  1096→				bi.batchSize = c
  1097→
  1098→				done := errors.Is(batchErr, ebpf.ErrKeyNotExist)
  1099→				// Lookup batch on LRU hash map may fail if the buffer passed is not big enough to
  1100→				// accommodate the largest bucket size in the LRU map [1]
  1101→				// Because bucket size, in general, cannot be known, we approximate a good starting
  1102→				// buffer size from the approximation of how many entries there should be in the map
  1103→				// before expect to see a hash map collision: sqrt(max_entries * 2)
  1104→				//
  1105→				// If we receive ENOSPC failures, we will try to recover by growing the batch buffer
  1106→				// size (up to some max number of retries - default: 3) and retrying the iteration.
  1107→				//
  1108→				// [1] https://elixir.bootlin.com/linux/v6.12.6/source/kernel/bpf/hashtab.c#L1807-L1809
  1109→				//
  1110→				// Note: If this failure happens during the bpf syscall, it is expected that the underlying
  1111→				// cursor will not have been swapped - meaning that we can retry the iteration at the same cursor.
  1112→				if errors.Is(batchErr, unix.ENOSPC) {
  1113→					if retry == bi.maxBatchedRetries()-1 {
  1114→						bi.err = batchErr
  1115→					} else {
  1116→						bi.chunkSize *= 2
  1117→						bi.keys = make([]KT, bi.chunkSize)
  1118→						bi.vals = make([]VT, bi.chunkSize)
  1119→					}
  1120→					continue retry
  1121→				} else if !done && batchErr != nil {
  1122→					// If we're not done, and we didn't hit a ENOSPC then stop iteration and record
  1123→					// the error.
  1124→					bi.err = fmt.Errorf("failed to iterate map: %w", batchErr)
  1125→					return
  1126→				}
  1127→
  1128→				// Yield all received pairs.
  1129→				for i := range bi.batchSize {
  1130→					processed++
  1131→					if !yield(&bi.keys[i], &bi.vals[i]) {
  1132→						break iterate
  1133→					}
  1134→				}
  1135→
  1136→				if done {
  1137→					break iterate
  1138→				}
  1139→				break retry // finish retry loop for this batch.
  1140→			}
  1141→		}
  1142→	}
  1143→}

  1145→// Dump returns the map (type map[string][]string) which contains all
  1146→// data stored in BPF map.
  1147→func (m *Map) Dump(hash map[string][]string) error {
  1148→	callback := func(key MapKey, value MapValue) {
  1149→		// No need to deep copy since we are creating strings.
  1150→		hash[key.String()] = append(hash[key.String()], value.String())
  1151→	}
  1152→
  1153→	if err := m.DumpWithCallback(callback); err != nil {
  1154→		return err
  1155→	}
  1156→
  1157→	return nil
  1158→}
  1159→
  1160→// BatchLookup returns the count of elements in the map by dumping the map
  1161→// using batch lookup.

  1163→	return m.m.BatchLookup(cursor, keysOut, valuesOut, opts)

  1165→
  1166→// DumpIfExists dumps the contents of the map into hash via Dump() if the map

  1171→		return err
  1172→	}

  1174→	if found {

  1179→}

  1181→func (m *Map) Lookup(key MapKey) (MapValue, error) {
  1182→	if err := m.Open(); err != nil {
  1183→		return nil, err
  1184→	}
  1185→
  1186→	m.lock.RLock()
  1187→	defer m.lock.RUnlock()
  1188→
  1189→	var duration *spanstat.SpanStat
  1190→	if metrics.BPFSyscallDuration.IsEnabled() {
  1191→		duration = spanstat.Start()
  1192→	}
  1193→
  1194→	value := m.value.New()
  1195→	err := m.m.Lookup(key, value)
  1196→

  1198→		metrics.BPFSyscallDuration.WithLabelValues(metricOpLookup, metrics.Error2Outcome(err)).Observe(duration.End(err == nil).Total().Seconds())
  1199→	}
  1200→
  1201→	if err != nil {
  1202→		return nil, err
  1203→	}
  1204→
  1205→	return value, nil
  1206→}
  1207→
  1208→func (m *Map) Update(key MapKey, value MapValue) error {
  1209→	var err error
  1210→
  1211→	m.lock.Lock()
  1212→	defer m.lock.Unlock()
  1213→
  1214→	defer func() {
  1215→		desiredAction := OK
  1216→		if err != nil {
  1217→			desiredAction = Insert
  1218→		}
  1219→		entry := &cacheEntry{
  1220→			Key:           key,
  1221→			Value:         value,
  1222→			DesiredAction: desiredAction,
  1223→			LastError:     err,
  1224→		}
  1225→		m.addToEventsLocked(MapUpdate, *entry)
  1226→
  1227→		if m.cache == nil {
  1228→			return
  1229→		}
  1230→
  1231→		if m.withValueCache {
  1232→			if err != nil {
  1233→				m.scheduleErrorResolver()
  1234→			}
  1235→			m.cache[key.String()] = &cacheEntry{
  1236→				Key:           key,
  1237→				Value:         value,
  1238→				DesiredAction: desiredAction,
  1239→				LastError:     err,
  1240→			}
  1241→			m.updatePressureMetric()
  1242→		} else if err == nil {
  1243→			m.cache[key.String()] = nil
  1244→			m.updatePressureMetric()
  1245→		}
  1246→	}()
  1247→
  1248→	if err = m.open(); err != nil {
  1249→		return err
  1250→	}
  1251→
  1252→	err = m.m.Update(key, value, ebpf.UpdateAny)
  1253→
  1254→	if metrics.BPFMapOps.IsEnabled() {
  1255→		metrics.BPFMapOps.WithLabelValues(m.commonName(), metricOpUpdate, metrics.Error2Outcome(err)).Inc()
  1256→	}
  1257→
  1258→	if err != nil {
  1259→		return fmt.Errorf("update map %s: %w", m.Name(), err)
  1260→	}
  1261→
  1262→	return nil
  1263→}
  1264→
  1265→// deleteMapEvent is run at every delete map event.
  1266→// If cache is enabled, it will update the cache to reflect the delete.
  1267→// As well, if event buffer is enabled, it adds a new event to the buffer.
  1268→func (m *Map) deleteMapEvent(key MapKey, err error) {

  1270→		Key:           key,
  1271→		DesiredAction: Delete,
  1272→		LastError:     err,
  1273→	})
  1274→	m.deleteCacheEntry(key, err)
  1275→}
  1276→
  1277→func (m *Map) deleteAllMapEvent() {
  1278→	m.addToEventsLocked(MapDeleteAll, cacheEntry{})

  1280→
  1281→// deleteCacheEntry evaluates the specified error, if nil the map key is
  1282→// removed from the cache to indicate successful deletion. If non-nil, the map
  1283→// key entry in the cache is updated to indicate deletion failure with the
  1284→// specified error.
  1285→//
  1286→// Caller must hold m.lock for writing
  1287→func (m *Map) deleteCacheEntry(key MapKey, err error) {
  1288→	if m.cache == nil {
  1289→		return
  1290→	}
  1291→
  1292→	k := key.String()
  1293→	if err == nil {
  1294→		delete(m.cache, k)
  1295→	} else if !m.withValueCache {
  1296→		return
  1297→	} else {
  1298→		entry, ok := m.cache[k]
  1299→		if !ok {
  1300→			m.cache[k] = &cacheEntry{
  1301→				Key: key,
  1302→			}
  1303→			entry = m.cache[k]
  1304→		}
  1305→
  1306→		entry.DesiredAction = Delete
  1307→		entry.LastError = err
  1308→		m.scheduleErrorResolver()
  1309→	}
  1310→}
  1311→
  1312→// delete deletes the map entry corresponding to the given key. If ignoreMissing
  1313→// is set to true and the entry was not found, the error metric is not
  1314→// incremented for missing entries and nil error is returned.
  1315→func (m *Map) delete(key MapKey, ignoreMissing bool) (_ bool, err error) {
  1316→	defer func() {
  1317→		m.deleteMapEvent(key, err)
  1318→		if err != nil {
  1319→			m.updatePressureMetric()
  1320→		}
  1321→	}()
  1322→
  1323→	if err = m.open(); err != nil {
  1324→		return false, err
  1325→	}
  1326→
  1327→	var duration *spanstat.SpanStat
  1328→	if metrics.BPFSyscallDuration.IsEnabled() {
  1329→		duration = spanstat.Start()
  1330→	}
  1331→
  1332→	err = m.m.Delete(key)
  1333→
  1334→	if metrics.BPFSyscallDuration.IsEnabled() {
  1335→		metrics.BPFSyscallDuration.WithLabelValues(metricOpDelete, metrics.Error2Outcome(err)).Observe(duration.End(err == nil).Total().Seconds())
  1336→	}
  1337→
  1338→	if errors.Is(err, ebpf.ErrKeyNotExist) && ignoreMissing {
  1339→		// Error and metrics handling is skipped in case ignoreMissing is set and
  1340→		// the map key did not exist. This removes false positives in the delete
  1341→		// metrics and skips the deferred cleanup of nonexistent entries. This
  1342→		// situation occurs at least in the context of cleanup of NAT mappings from
  1343→		// CT GC.
  1344→		return false, nil
  1345→	}
  1346→

  1348→		// err can be nil or any error other than ebpf.ErrKeyNotExist.
  1349→		metrics.BPFMapOps.WithLabelValues(m.commonName(), metricOpDelete, metrics.Error2Outcome(err)).Inc()
  1350→	}
  1351→
  1352→	if err != nil {
  1353→		return false, fmt.Errorf("unable to delete element %s from map %s: %w", key, m.name, err)
  1354→	}

  1357→}

  1359→// SilentDelete deletes the map entry corresponding to the given key.
  1360→// If a map entry is not found this returns (false, nil).

  1367→
  1368→// Delete deletes the map entry corresponding to the given key.

  1387→// DeleteAll deletes all entries of a map by traversing the map and deleting individual
  1388→// entries. Note that if entries are added while the taversal is in progress,
  1389→// such entries may survive the deletion process.
  1390→func (m *Map) DeleteAll() error {

  1392→	defer m.lock.Unlock()

  1394→	m.Logger.Debug("deleting all entries in map")
  1395→

  1397→		// Mark all entries for deletion, upon successful deletion,
  1398→		// entries will be removed or the LastError will be updated

  1400→			entry.DesiredAction = Delete
  1401→			entry.LastError = fmt.Errorf("deletion pending")
  1402→		}
  1403→	}

  1405→	if err := m.open(); err != nil {
  1406→		return err
  1407→	}
  1408→
  1409→	mk := m.key.New()
  1410→	mv := make([]byte, m.ValueSize())
  1411→
  1412→	defer m.deleteAllMapEvent()
  1413→
  1414→	i := m.m.Iterate()
  1415→	for i.Next(mk, &mv) {
  1416→		err := m.m.Delete(mk)
  1417→
  1418→		m.deleteCacheEntry(mk, err)
  1419→
  1420→		if err != nil {
  1421→			return err
  1422→		}
  1423→	}
  1424→
  1425→	err := i.Err()
  1426→	if err != nil {
  1427→		m.Logger.Warn(
  1428→			"Unable to correlate iteration key with cache entry. Inconsistent cache.",
  1429→			logfields.Error, err,
  1430→			logfields.Key, mk,
  1431→		)
  1432→	}
  1433→
  1434→	return err
  1435→}
  1436→
  1437→func (m *Map) ClearAll() error {
  1438→	if m.eventsBufferEnabled || m.withValueCache {
  1439→		return fmt.Errorf("clear map: events buffer and value cache are not supported")
  1440→	}
  1441→
  1442→	m.lock.Lock()
  1443→	defer m.lock.Unlock()
  1444→	defer m.updatePressureMetric()
  1445→
  1446→	if err := m.open(); err != nil {
  1447→		return err
  1448→	}
  1449→
  1450→	mk := m.key.New()
  1451→	var mv any
  1452→	if m.hasPerCPUValue() {
  1453→		mv = m.value.(MapPerCPUValue).NewSlice()
  1454→	} else {
  1455→		mv = m.value.New()
  1456→	}
  1457→	empty := reflect.Indirect(reflect.ValueOf(mv)).Interface()
  1458→
  1459→	i := m.m.Iterate()
  1460→	for i.Next(mk, mv) {
  1461→		err := m.m.Update(mk, empty, ebpf.UpdateAny)
  1462→
  1463→		if metrics.BPFMapOps.IsEnabled() {
  1464→			metrics.BPFMapOps.WithLabelValues(m.commonName(), metricOpUpdate, metrics.Error2Outcome(err)).Inc()
  1465→		}
  1466→
  1467→		if err != nil {
  1468→			return err
  1469→		}
  1470→	}
  1471→
  1472→	return i.Err()
  1473→}

  1475→// GetModel returns a BPF map in the representation served via the API
  1476→func (m *Map) GetModel() *models.BPFMap {
  1477→
  1478→	mapModel := &models.BPFMap{
  1479→		Path: m.path,
  1480→	}
  1481→

  1483→	if m.withValueCache {
  1484→		m.lock.RLock()
  1485→		defer m.lock.RUnlock()
  1486→		for k, entry := range m.cache {
  1487→			model := &models.BPFMapEntry{
  1488→				Key:           k,
  1489→				DesiredAction: entry.DesiredAction.String(),
  1490→			}
  1491→
  1492→			if entry.LastError != nil {
  1493→				model.LastError = entry.LastError.Error()
  1494→			}
  1495→
  1496→			if entry.Value != nil {
  1497→				model.Value = entry.Value.String()

  1503→
  1504→	stats := NewDumpStats(m)

  1506→		mapModel.Cache = append(mapModel.Cache, &models.BPFMapEntry{
  1507→			Key:   key.String(),
  1508→			Value: value.String(),

  1516→func (m *Map) addToEventsLocked(action Action, entry cacheEntry) {
  1517→	if !m.eventsBufferEnabled {
  1518→		return
  1519→	}
  1520→	m.events.add(&Event{
  1521→		action:     action,
  1522→		Timestamp:  time.Now(),
  1523→		cacheEntry: entry,
  1524→	})
  1525→}
  1526→
  1527→// resolveErrors is schedule by scheduleErrorResolver() and runs periodically.
  1528→// It resolves up to maxSyncErrors discrepancies between cache and BPF map in
  1529→// the kernel.
  1530→func (m *Map) resolveErrors(ctx context.Context) error {
  1531→	started := time.Now()
  1532→
  1533→	m.lock.Lock()
  1534→	defer m.lock.Unlock()
  1535→
  1536→	if m.cache == nil {
  1537→		return nil
  1538→	}
  1539→
  1540→	if !m.outstandingErrors {
  1541→		return nil
  1542→	}
  1543→
  1544→	outstanding := 0
  1545→	for _, e := range m.cache {
  1546→		switch e.DesiredAction {
  1547→		case Insert, Delete:
  1548→			outstanding++
  1549→		}
  1550→	}
  1551→
  1552→	// Errors appear to have already been resolved. This can happen if a subsequent
  1553→	// Update/Delete operation acting on the same key succeeded.
  1554→	if outstanding == 0 {
  1555→		m.outstandingErrors = false
  1556→		return nil
  1557→	}
  1558→
  1559→	if err := m.open(); err != nil {
  1560→		return err
  1561→	}
  1562→
  1563→	m.Logger.Debug(
  1564→		"Starting periodic BPF map error resolver",
  1565→		logfields.Remaining, outstanding,
  1566→	)
  1567→
  1568→	resolved := 0
  1569→	scanned := 0
  1570→	nerr := 0
  1571→	for k, e := range m.cache {
  1572→		scanned++
  1573→
  1574→		switch e.DesiredAction {
  1575→		case OK:
  1576→		case Insert:
  1577→			// Call into ebpf-go's Map.Update() directly, don't go through the cache.
  1578→			err := m.m.Update(e.Key, e.Value, ebpf.UpdateAny)
  1579→			if metrics.BPFMapOps.IsEnabled() {
  1580→				metrics.BPFMapOps.WithLabelValues(m.commonName(), metricOpUpdate, metrics.Error2Outcome(err)).Inc()
  1581→			}
  1582→			if err == nil {
  1583→				e.DesiredAction = OK
  1584→				e.LastError = nil
  1585→				resolved++
  1586→				outstanding--
  1587→			} else {
  1588→				e.LastError = err
  1589→				nerr++
  1590→			}
  1591→			m.cache[k] = e
  1592→			m.addToEventsLocked(MapUpdate, *e)
  1593→		case Delete:
  1594→			// Holding lock, issue direct delete on map.
  1595→			err := m.m.Delete(e.Key)
  1596→			if metrics.BPFMapOps.IsEnabled() {
  1597→				metrics.BPFMapOps.WithLabelValues(m.commonName(), metricOpDelete, metrics.Error2Outcome(err)).Inc()
  1598→			}
  1599→			if err == nil || errors.Is(err, ebpf.ErrKeyNotExist) {
  1600→				delete(m.cache, k)
  1601→				resolved++
  1602→				outstanding--
  1603→			} else {
  1604→				e.LastError = err
  1605→				nerr++
  1606→				m.cache[k] = e
  1607→			}
  1608→
  1609→			m.addToEventsLocked(MapDelete, *e)
  1610→		}
  1611→
  1612→		// bail out if maximum errors are reached to relax the map lock
  1613→		if nerr > maxSyncErrors {
  1614→			break
  1615→		}
  1616→	}
  1617→
  1618→	m.updatePressureMetric()
  1619→
  1620→	m.Logger.Debug(
  1621→		"BPF map error resolver completed",
  1622→		logfields.Remaining, outstanding,
  1623→		logfields.Resolved, resolved,
  1624→		logfields.Scanned, scanned,
  1625→		logfields.Duration, time.Since(started),
  1626→	)
  1627→
  1628→	m.outstandingErrors = outstanding > 0
  1629→	if m.outstandingErrors {
  1630→		return fmt.Errorf("%d map sync errors", outstanding)
  1631→	}
  1632→
  1633→	return nil
  1634→}
  1635→
  1636→func (m *Map) exist() (bool, error) {
  1637→	path, err := m.Path()
  1638→	if err != nil {
  1639→		return false, err
  1640→	}
  1641→
  1642→	if _, err := os.Stat(path); err == nil {
  1643→		return true, nil
  1644→	}
  1645→
  1646→	return false, nil
  1647→}

   142→// BatchCount the number of elements in the map using a batch lookup.
   143→// Only usable for hash, lru-hash and lpm-trie maps.
   144→func (m *Map) BatchCount() (count int, err error) {
   145→	switch m.Type() {
   146→	case ebpf.Hash, ebpf.LRUHash, ebpf.LPMTrie:
   147→		break
   148→	default:
   149→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("unsupported map type %s, must be one either hash or lru-hash types", m.Type())
   150→	}
   151→	chunkSize := startingChunkSize(int(m.MaxEntries()))
   152→
   153→	// Since we don't care about the actual data we just use a no-op binary
   154→	// decoder.
   155→	keys := make(nopDecoder, chunkSize)
   156→	vals := make(nopDecoder, chunkSize)
   157→	maxRetries := defaultBatchedRetries
   158→
   159→	var cursor ebpf.MapBatchCursor
   160→	for {
   161→		for retry := range maxRetries {
   162→			// Attempt to read batch into buffer.
   163→			c, batchErr := m.BatchLookup(&cursor, keys, vals, nil)
   164→			count += c
   165→
   166→			switch {
   167→			// Lookup batch on LRU hash map may fail if the buffer passed is not big enough to
   168→			// accommodate the largest bucket size in the LRU map. See full comment in
   169→			// [BatchIterator.IterateAll]
   170→			case errors.Is(batchErr, unix.ENOSPC):
   171→				if retry == maxRetries-1 {
   172→					err = batchErr
   173→				} else {
   174→					chunkSize *= 2
   175→				}
   176→				keys = make(nopDecoder, chunkSize)
   177→				vals = make(nopDecoder, chunkSize)
   178→				continue
   179→			case errors.Is(batchErr, ebpf.ErrKeyNotExist):
   180→				return
   181→			case batchErr != nil:
   182→				// If we're not done, and we didn't hit a ENOSPC then stop iteration and record
   183→				// the error.
   184→				err = fmt.Errorf("failed to iterate map: %w", batchErr)
   185→				return
   186→			}
   187→			// Do the next batch
   188→			break
   189→		}
   190→	}
   191→}

   303→func (m *Map) commonName() string {
   304→	if m.cachedCommonName != "" {
   305→		return m.cachedCommonName
   306→	}
   307→
   308→	m.cachedCommonName = extractCommonName(m.name)
   309→	return m.cachedCommonName
   310→}
   311→
   312→func (m *Map) NonPrefixedName() string {
   313→	return strings.TrimPrefix(m.name, metrics.Namespace+"_")
   314→}
   315→
   316→// scheduleErrorResolver schedules a periodic resolver controller that scans
   317→// all BPF map caches for unresolved errors and attempts to resolve them. On
   318→// error of resolution, the controller is-rescheduled in an expedited manner
   319→// with an exponential back-off.
   320→//
   321→// m.lock must be held for writing
   322→func (m *Map) scheduleErrorResolver() {
   323→	m.outstandingErrors = true
   324→
   325→	if time.Since(m.errorResolverLastScheduled) <= errorResolverSchedulerMinInterval {
   326→		return
   327→	}
   328→
   329→	m.errorResolverLastScheduled = time.Now()
   330→
   331→	go func() {
   332→		time.Sleep(errorResolverSchedulerDelay)
   333→		mapControllers.UpdateController(m.controllerName(),
   334→			controller.ControllerParams{
   335→				Group:       bpfMapSyncControllerGroup,
   336→				DoFunc:      m.resolveErrors,
   337→				RunInterval: errorResolverSchedulerMinInterval,
   338→			},
   339→		)
   340→	}()
   341→
   342→}
   343→
   344→// WithCache enables use of a cache. This will store all entries inserted from
   345→// user space in a local cache (map) and will indicate the status of each
   346→// individual entry.
   347→func (m *Map) WithCache() *Map {
   348→	if m.cache == nil {
   349→		m.cache = map[string]*cacheEntry{}
   350→	}
   351→	m.withValueCache = true
   352→	m.enableSync = true
   353→	return m
   354→}
   355→
   356→// WithEvents enables use of the event buffer, if the buffer is enabled.
   357→// This stores all map events (i.e. add/update/delete) in a bounded event buffer.
   358→// If eventTTL is not zero, then events that are older than the TTL
   359→// will periodically be removed from the buffer.
   360→// Enabling events will use approx proportional to 100MB for every million capacity
   361→// in maxSize.
   362→//
   363→// TODO: The IPCache map have many periodic update events added by a controller for entries such as the 0.0.0.0/0 range.
   364→// These fill the event buffer with possibly unnecessary events.
   365→// We should either provide an option to aggregate these events, ignore hem from the ipcache event buffer or store them in a separate buffer.
   366→func (m *Map) WithEvents(c option.BPFEventBufferConfig) *Map {
   367→	if !c.Enabled {
   368→		return m
   369→	}
   370→	m.Logger.Debug(
   371→		"enabling events buffer",
   372→		logfields.Size, c.MaxSize,

1→// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
     2→// Copyright Authors of Cilium
     3→
     4→package bpf
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"context"
     8→	"errors"
     9→	"fmt"
    10→	"log/slog"
    11→
    12→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/container"
    13→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/controller"
    14→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields"
    15→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/time"
    16→
    17→	"github.com/cilium/stream"
    18→)
    19→
    20→// Action describes an action for map buffer events.
    21→type Action uint8
    22→
    23→const (
    24→	// MapUpdate describes a map.Update event.
    25→	MapUpdate Action = iota
    26→	// MapDelete describes a map.Delete event.
    27→	MapDelete
    28→	// MapDeleteAll describes a map.DeleteAll event which is aggregated into a single event
    29→	// to minimize memory and subscription buffer usage.
    30→	MapDeleteAll
    31→)
    32→
    33→var bpfEventBufferGCControllerGroup = controller.NewGroup("bpf-event-buffer-gc")
    34→
    35→// String returns a string representation of an Action.
    36→func (e Action) String() string {
    37→	switch e {
    38→	case MapUpdate:
    39→		return "update"
    40→	case MapDelete:
    41→		return "delete"
    42→	case MapDeleteAll:
    43→		return "delete-all"
    44→	default:
    45→		return "unknown"
    46→	}
    47→}
    48→
    49→// Event contains data about a bpf operation event.
    50→type Event struct {
    51→	Timestamp time.Time
    52→	action    Action
    53→	cacheEntry
    54→}
    55→
    56→// GetAction returns the event action string.
    57→func (e *Event) GetAction() string {
    58→	return e.action.String()
    59→}
    60→
    61→// GetKey returns the string representation of a event key.
    62→func (e Event) GetKey() string {
    63→	if e.cacheEntry.Key == nil {
    64→		return "<nil>"
    65→	}
    66→	return e.cacheEntry.Key.String()
    67→}
    68→
    69→// GetValue returns the string representation of a event value.
    70→// Nil values (such as with deletes) are returned as a canonical
    71→// string representation.
    72→func (e Event) GetValue() string {
    73→	if e.cacheEntry.Value == nil {
    74→		return "<nil>"
    75→	}
    76→	return e.cacheEntry.Value.String()
    77→}
    78→
    79→// GetLastError returns the last error for an event.
    80→func (e Event) GetLastError() error {
    81→	return e.cacheEntry.LastError
    82→}
    83→
    84→// GetDesiredAction returns the desired action enum for an event.
    85→func (e Event) GetDesiredAction() DesiredAction {
    86→	return e.cacheEntry.DesiredAction
    87→}
    88→
    89→func newEventsBuffer(logger *slog.Logger, name string, bufSize int, ttl time.Duration) *eventsBuffer {
    90→	b := &eventsBuffer{
    91→		logger:   logger,
    92→		buffer:   container.NewRingBuffer[Event](bufSize),
    93→		eventTTL: ttl,
    94→	}
    95→	b.observe, b.next, b.done = stream.Multicast[Event]()
    96→	b.observe.Observe(context.Background(), func(e Event) {
    97→		b.buffer.Add(e)
    98→	}, func(err error) {})
    99→	if b.eventTTL > 0 {
   100→		logger.Debug("starting bpf map event buffer GC controller")
   101→		mapControllers.UpdateController(
   102→			fmt.Sprintf("bpf-event-buffer-gc-%s", name),
   103→			controller.ControllerParams{
   104→				Group: bpfEventBufferGCControllerGroup,
   105→				DoFunc: func(_ context.Context) error {
   106→					logger.Debug(
   107→						"clearing bpf map events older than TTL",
   108→						logfields.TTL, b.eventTTL,
   109→					)
   110→					b.buffer.Compact(func(event Event) bool {
   111→						return time.Since(event.Timestamp) < b.eventTTL
   112→					})
   113→					return nil
   114→				},
   115→				RunInterval: b.eventTTL,
   116→			},
   117→		)
   118→	}
   119→	return b
   120→}
   121→
   122→func (m *Map) initEventsBuffer(maxSize int, eventsTTL time.Duration) {
   123→	m.events = newEventsBuffer(m.Logger, m.name, maxSize, eventsTTL)
   124→}
   125→
   126→// eventsBuffer stores a buffer of events for auditing and debugging
   127→// purposes.
   128→type eventsBuffer struct {
   129→	logger   *slog.Logger
   130→	buffer   *container.RingBuffer[Event]
   131→	eventTTL time.Duration
   132→
   133→	observe stream.Observable[Event]
   134→	next    func(Event)
   135→	done    func(error)
   136→}
   137→
   138→func (eb *eventsBuffer) dumpAndSubscribe(ctx context.Context, callback EventCallbackFunc, follow bool) {
   139→	eb.dumpWithCallback(callback)
   140→	if follow {
   141→		eb.observe.Observe(ctx, callback, func(err error) {
   142→			if errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
   143→				eb.logger.Debug("map event observable cancelled", logfields.Error, err)
   144→				return
   145→			}
   146→			eb.logger.Error("failed while observing map events", logfields.Error, err)
   147→		})
   148→	}
   149→}
   150→

   129→	logger   *slog.Logger
   130→	buffer   *container.RingBuffer[Event]
   131→	eventTTL time.Duration
   132→
   133→	observe stream.Observable[Event]
   134→	next    func(Event)
   135→	done    func(error)
   136→}
   137→
   138→func (eb *eventsBuffer) dumpAndSubscribe(ctx context.Context, callback EventCallbackFunc, follow bool) {
   139→	eb.dumpWithCallback(callback)
   140→	if follow {
   141→		eb.observe.Observe(ctx, callback, func(err error) {
   142→			if errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
   143→				eb.logger.Debug("map event observable cancelled", logfields.Error, err)
   144→				return
   145→			}
   146→			eb.logger.Error("failed while observing map events", logfields.Error, err)
   147→		})
   148→	}
   149→}

1→// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
     2→// Copyright Authors of Cilium
     3→
     4→package bpf
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"errors"
     8→	"fmt"
     9→	"log/slog"
    10→	"os"
    11→	"path"
    12→
    13→	"github.com/cilium/ebpf"
    14→	"golang.org/x/sys/unix"
    15→
    16→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields"
    17→)
    18→
    19→// Cilium-specific pinning flags used in bpf C code to request specific pinning
    20→// behaviour from the agent.
    21→const (
    22→	// PinAlwaysReplace matches CILIUM_PIN_REPLACE.
    23→	PinReplace = ebpf.PinType(1 << 4)
    24→)
    25→
    26→// consumePinReplace returns the key names of MapSpecs in spec with the
    27→// CILIUM_PIN_REPLACE pinning flag set. Clears the CILIUM_PIN_REPLACE flag.
    28→func consumePinReplace(spec *ebpf.CollectionSpec) []string {
    29→	var toReplace []string
    30→	for key, ms := range spec.Maps {
    31→		if ms.Pinning == PinReplace {
    32→			toReplace = append(toReplace, key)
    33→			ms.Pinning = 0
    34→		}
    35→	}
    36→	return toReplace
    37→}
    38→
    39→// incompatibleMaps returns the key names MapSpecs in spec with the
    40→// LIBBPF_PIN_BY_NAME pinning flag that are incompatible with their pinned
    41→// counterparts. Removes the LIBBPF_PIN_BY_NAME flag. opts.Maps.PinPath must be
    42→// specified.
    43→//
    44→// The slice of strings returned contains the keys used in Collection.Maps and
    45→// CollectionSpec.Maps, which can differ from the Map's Name field.
    46→func incompatibleMaps(spec *ebpf.CollectionSpec, opts ebpf.CollectionOptions) ([]string, error) {
    47→	if opts.Maps.PinPath == "" {
    48→		return nil, errors.New("missing opts.Maps.PinPath")
    49→	}
    50→
    51→	var incompatible []string
    52→	for key, ms := range spec.Maps {
    53→		if ms.Pinning != ebpf.PinByName {
    54→			continue
    55→		}
    56→
    57→		pinPath := path.Join(opts.Maps.PinPath, ms.Name)
    58→		m, err := ebpf.LoadPinnedMap(pinPath, nil)
    59→		if errors.Is(err, unix.ENOENT) {
    60→			continue
    61→		}
    62→		if err != nil {
    63→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("opening map %s from pin: %w", ms.Name, err)
    64→		}
    65→
    66→		if ms.Compatible(m) == nil {
    67→			m.Close()
    68→			continue
    69→		}
    70→
    71→		incompatible = append(incompatible, key)
    72→		ms.Pinning = 0
    73→	}
    74→
    75→	return incompatible, nil
    76→}
    77→
    78→type toPin struct {
    79→	m    *ebpf.Map
    80→	path string
    81→}
    82→
    83→// mapsToReplace returns a slice of paths and ebpf.Maps corresponding to the
    84→// entries provided in toReplace. Commit the returned pins after attaching the
    85→// Collection's programs to the required hooks using [commitMapPins].
    86→//
    87→// This has two main purposes: replacing pins of incompatible maps that are
    88→// upgraded or downgraded by a different version of the agent, as well as
    89→// replacing pins of maps that should never be reused/repopulated by the loader,
    90→// like tail call maps.
    91→//
    92→// Letting the loader repopulate an existing tail call map will transition the
    93→// program through invalid states. For example, code can be moved from one tail
    94→// call to another, making some instructions execute twice or not at all
    95→// depending on the order the tail calls were inserted.
    96→func mapsToReplace(toReplace []string, spec *ebpf.CollectionSpec, coll *ebpf.Collection, opts ebpf.CollectionOptions) ([]toPin, error) {
    97→	if opts.Maps.PinPath == "" && len(toReplace) > 0 {
    98→		return nil, errors.New("empty Maps.PinPath in CollectionOptions")
    99→	}
   100→
   101→	// We need both Map and MapSpec as the pin path is derived from MapSpec.Name,
   102→	// and can be modified before creating the Collection. Maps are often renamed
   103→	// to give them unique bpffs pin paths. MapInfo is/was truncated to 20 chars,
   104→	// so we need the MapSpec.Name.
   105→	var pins []toPin
   106→	for _, key := range toReplace {
   107→		m, ok := coll.Maps[key]
   108→		if !ok {
   109→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("Map %s not found in Collection", key)
   110→		}
   111→		ms, ok := spec.Maps[key]
   112→		if !ok {
   113→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("MapSpec %s not found in CollectionSpec", key)
   114→		}
   115→
   116→		if m.IsPinned() {
   117→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("Map %s was already pinned by ebpf-go: LIBBPF_PIN_BY_NAME and CILIUM_PIN_REPLACE are mutually exclusive", ms.Name)
   118→		}
   119→
   120→		pins = append(pins, toPin{m: m, path: path.Join(opts.Maps.PinPath, ms.Name)})
   121→	}
   122→
   123→	return pins, nil
   124→}
   125→
   126→// commitMapPins removes the given map pins and replaces them with the
   127→// corresponding Maps. This is to be called after the Collection's programs have
   128→// been attached to the required hooks. Any existing pins are overwritten.
   129→func commitMapPins(logger *slog.Logger, pins []toPin) error {
   130→	for _, pin := range pins {
   131→		if err := os.Remove(pin.path); err != nil && !errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
   132→			return fmt.Errorf("removing map pin at %s: %w", pin.path, err)
   133→		}
   134→		if err := pin.m.Pin(pin.path); err != nil {
   135→			return fmt.Errorf("pinning map to %s: %w", pin.path, err)
   136→		}
   137→
   138→		logger.Debug("Replaced map pin", logfields.Pin, pin.path)
   139→	}
   140→
   141→	return nil
   142→}
   143→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

   120→		pins = append(pins, toPin{m: m, path: path.Join(opts.Maps.PinPath, ms.Name)})
   121→	}
   122→
   123→	return pins, nil
   124→}
   125→
   126→// commitMapPins removes the given map pins and replaces them with the
   127→// corresponding Maps. This is to be called after the Collection's programs have
   128→// been attached to the required hooks. Any existing pins are overwritten.
   129→func commitMapPins(logger *slog.Logger, pins []toPin) error {
   130→	for _, pin := range pins {
   131→		if err := os.Remove(pin.path); err != nil && !errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
   132→			return fmt.Errorf("removing map pin at %s: %w", pin.path, err)
   133→		}
   134→		if err := pin.m.Pin(pin.path); err != nil {
   135→			return fmt.Errorf("pinning map to %s: %w", pin.path, err)
   136→		}
   137→
   138→		logger.Debug("Replaced map pin", logfields.Pin, pin.path)
   139→	}
   140→
   141→	return nil
   142→}
   143→

   912→// BatchIterator provides a typed wrapper *Map that allows for batched iteration
   913→// of bpf maps.
   914→type BatchIterator[KT, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]] struct {
   915→	m    *Map
   916→	err  error
   917→	keys []KT
   918→	vals []VT
   919→
   920→	chunkSize      int
   921→	maxDumpRetries uint32
   922→
   923→	// Iteration stats
   924→	batchSize int
   925→
   926→	opts *ebpf.BatchOptions
   927→}
   928→
   929→// NewBatchIterator that allows for iterating a map using the bpf batch api.
   930→// This automatically handles concerns such as batch sizing and handling errors
   931→// when end of map is reached.
   932→// Following iteration, any unresolved errors encountered when iterating
   933→// the bpf map can be accessed via the Err() function.
   934→// The pointer type of KT & VT must implement Map{Key,Value}, respectively.
   935→//
   936→// Subsequent iterations via IterateAll reset all internal state and begin
   937→// iteration over.
   938→//
   939→// Example usage:
   940→//
   941→//	m := NewMap("cilium_test",
   942→//		ebpf.Hash,
   943→//		&TestKey{}, 	// *TestKey implements MapKey.
   944→//		&TestValue{}, 	// *TestValue implements MapValue.
   945→//		mapSize,
   946→//		BPF_F_NO_PREALLOC,
   947→//	)
   948→//
   949→//	iter := NewBatchIterator[TestKey, TestValue](m)
   950→//	for k, v := range iter.IterateAll(context.TODO()) {
   951→//		// ...
   952→//	}
   953→func NewBatchIterator[KT any, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]](m *Map) *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP] {
   954→	return &BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]{
   955→		m: m,
   956→	}
   957→}
   958→
   959→// KeyPointer is a generic interface that provides the constraint that the pointer
   960→// of a type T is a MapKey.
   961→type KeyPointer[T any] interface {
   962→	MapKey
   963→	*T
   964→}
   965→
   966→// ValuePointer is a generic interface that provides the constraint that the pointer
   967→// of a type T is a MapValue.
   968→type ValuePointer[T any] interface {
   969→	MapValue
   970→	*T
   971→}
   972→
   973→// Err returns errors encountered during the previous iteration when
   974→// IterateAll(...) is called.
   975→//
   976→// If the iterator is reused, the error will be reset,
   977→func (kvs BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) Err() error {
   978→	return kvs.err
   979→}
   980→
   981→func (bi BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) maxBatchedRetries() int {
   982→	if bi.maxDumpRetries > 0 {
   983→		return int(bi.maxDumpRetries)
   984→	}

   986→}
   987→
   988→const defaultBatchedRetries = 3

   990→type BatchIteratorOpt[KT any, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]] func(*BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]

  1031→func CountAll[KT, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]](ctx context.Context, iter *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) (int, error) {

   968→type ValuePointer[T any] interface {
   969→	MapValue
   970→	*T
   971→}
   972→
   973→// Err returns errors encountered during the previous iteration when
   974→// IterateAll(...) is called.
   975→//
   976→// If the iterator is reused, the error will be reset,
   977→func (kvs BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) Err() error {
   978→	return kvs.err
   979→}
   980→
   981→func (bi BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) maxBatchedRetries() int {
   982→	if bi.maxDumpRetries > 0 {
   983→		return int(bi.maxDumpRetries)
   984→	}
   985→	return defaultBatchedRetries
   986→}
   987→
   988→const defaultBatchedRetries = 3
   989→
   990→type BatchIteratorOpt[KT any, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]] func(*BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]
   991→
   992→// WithEBPFBatchOpts returns a batch iterator option that allows for overriding
   993→// BPF_MAP_LOOKUP_BATCH options.
   994→func WithEBPFBatchOpts[KT, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]](opts *ebpf.BatchOptions) BatchIteratorOpt[KT, VT, KP, VP] {
   995→	return func(in *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP] {
   996→		in.opts = opts
   997→		return in
   998→	}
   999→}
  1000→
  1001→// WithMaxRetries returns a batch iterator option that allows overriding the default
  1002→// max batch retries.
  1003→//
  1004→// Unless the starting chunk size is set to be the map size, it is possible for iteration
  1005→// to fail with ENOSPC if the passed allocated chunk array size is not big enough to accommodate
  1006→// a the bpf maps underlying hashmaps bucket size.
  1007→//
  1008→// If this happens, BatchIterator will automatically attempt to double the batch size and
  1009→// retry the iteration from the same place - up to a number of retries.
  1010→func WithMaxRetries[KT, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]](retries uint32) BatchIteratorOpt[KT, VT, KP, VP] {
  1011→	return func(in *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP] {
  1012→		in.maxDumpRetries = retries
  1013→		return in
  1014→	}
  1015→}
  1016→
  1017→// WithStartingChunkSize returns a batch iterator option that allows overriding the dynamically
  1018→// chosen starting chunk size.
  1019→func WithStartingChunkSize[KT, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]](size int) BatchIteratorOpt[KT, VT, KP, VP] {
  1020→	return func(in *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP] {
  1021→		in.chunkSize = size
  1022→		if in.chunkSize <= 0 {
  1023→			in.chunkSize = 8
  1024→		}
  1025→		return in
  1026→	}
  1027→}
  1028→
  1029→// CountAll is a helper function that returns the count of all elements in a batched
  1030→// iterator.
  1031→func CountAll[KT, VT any, KP KeyPointer[KT], VP ValuePointer[VT]](ctx context.Context, iter *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) (int, error) {
  1032→	c := 0
  1033→	for range iter.IterateAll(ctx) {
  1034→		c++
  1035→	}
  1036→	return c, iter.Err()
  1037→}
  1038→
  1039→func startingChunkSize(maxEntries int) int {
  1040→	bucketSize := math.Sqrt(float64(maxEntries * 2))
  1041→	nearest2 := math.Log2(bucketSize)
  1042→	return int(math.Pow(2, math.Ceil(nearest2)))
  1043→}

  1045→// IterateAll returns an iterate Seq2 type which can be used to iterate a map
  1046→// using the batched API.
  1047→// In the case of a the iteration failing due to insufficient batch buffer size,
  1048→// this will attempt to grow the buffer by a factor of 2 (up to a default: 3 amount
  1049→// of retries) and re-attempt the iteration.

  1051→// will be returned via Err().

  1053→// All other errors will result in immediate termination of iterator.
  1054→//
  1055→// If the iteration fails, then the Err() function will return the error that caused the failure.
  1056→func (bi *BatchIterator[KT, VT, KP, VP]) IterateAll(ctx context.Context, opts ...BatchIteratorOpt[KT, VT, KP, VP]) iter.Seq2[KP, VP] {
  1057→	switch bi.m.Type() {
  1058→	case ebpf.Hash, ebpf.LRUHash, ebpf.LPMTrie:
  1059→		break

  1061→		bi.err = fmt.Errorf("unsupported map type %s, must be one either hash or lru-hash types", bi.m.Type())
  1062→		return func(yield func(KP, VP) bool) {}
  1063→	}

  1065→	bi.chunkSize = startingChunkSize(int(bi.m.MaxEntries()))

  1069→			bi = opt(bi)

1→// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
     2→// Copyright Authors of Cilium
     3→
     4→//go:build linux
     5→
     6→package bpf
     7→
     8→import (
     9→	"errors"
    10→	"fmt"
    11→	"log/slog"
    12→	"os"
    13→	"path/filepath"
    14→	"sync"
    15→
    16→	"golang.org/x/sys/unix"
    17→
    18→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/components"
    19→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/defaults"
    20→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging"
    21→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields"
    22→	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/mountinfo"
    23→)
    24→
    25→var (
    26→	// Path to where bpffs is mounted
    27→	bpffsRoot = defaults.BPFFSRoot
    28→
    29→	// Set to true on first get request to detect misorder
    30→	lockedDown      = false
    31→	once            sync.Once
    32→	readMountInfo   sync.Once
    33→	mountInfoPrefix string
    34→)
    35→
    36→func lockDown() {
    37→	lockedDown = true
    38→}
    39→
    40→func setBPFFSRoot(path string) {
    41→	if lockedDown {
    42→		panic("setBPFFSRoot() call after bpffsRoot was read")
    43→	}
    44→	bpffsRoot = path
    45→}
    46→
    47→func BPFFSRoot() string {
    48→	once.Do(lockDown)
    49→	return bpffsRoot
    50→}
    51→
    52→// TCGlobalsPath returns the absolute path to <bpffs>/tc/globals, used for
    53→// legacy map pin paths.
    54→func TCGlobalsPath() string {
    55→	once.Do(lockDown)
    56→	return filepath.Join(bpffsRoot, defaults.TCGlobalsPath)
    57→}
    58→
    59→// CiliumPath returns the bpffs path to be used for Cilium object pins.
    60→func CiliumPath() string {
    61→	once.Do(lockDown)
    62→	return filepath.Join(bpffsRoot, "cilium")
    63→}
    64→
    65→// MkdirBPF wraps [os.MkdirAll] with the right permission bits for bpffs.
    66→// Use this for ensuring the existence of directories on bpffs.
    67→func MkdirBPF(path string) error {
    68→	return os.MkdirAll(path, 0755)
    69→}
    70→
    71→// Remove path ignoring ErrNotExist.
    72→func Remove(path string) error {
    73→	err := os.RemoveAll(path)
    74→	if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
    75→		return fmt.Errorf("removing bpffs directory at %s: %w", path, err)
    76→	}
    77→	return err
    78→}
    79→
    80→func tcPathFromMountInfo(logger *slog.Logger, name string) string {
    81→	readMountInfo.Do(func() {
    82→		mountInfos, err := mountinfo.GetMountInfo()
    83→		if err != nil {
    84→			logging.Fatal(logger, "Could not get mount info for map root lookup", logfields.Error, err)
    85→		}
    86→
    87→		for _, mountInfo := range mountInfos {
    88→			if mountInfo.FilesystemType == "bpf" {
    89→				mountInfoPrefix = filepath.Join(mountInfo.MountPoint, defaults.TCGlobalsPath)
    90→				return
    91→			}
    92→		}
    93→
    94→		logging.Fatal(logger, "Could not find BPF map root")
    95→	})
    96→
    97→	return filepath.Join(mountInfoPrefix, name)
    98→}
    99→
   100→// MapPath returns a path for a BPF map with a given name.
